Restaurant Inventory Analyzer
  • Data Analyzer (Second Half)

Data Analyzer (Second Half)

data_analyzer_second_half.py

Provides functions to analyze dish popularity and volume trends. Designed to be imported by main_gui.py.

data_analysis_package.data_analyzer_second_half.most_popular_dishes(data_frame1: DataFrame) → DataFrame[source]

Compute the most popular dishes based on frequency.

Parameters:

data_frame1 (pd.DataFrame) – Input DataFrame containing a column ‘NAME_x’ with dish names.

Returns:

DataFrame with: - ‘Name of dish’: unique dish names - ‘count’: number of occurrences of each dish Sorted in descending order of popularity.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

data_analysis_package.data_analyzer_second_half.volume_of_dishes(data_frame1: DataFrame) → DataFrame[source]

Compute the volume of dishes per day of the week.

Parameters:

data_frame1 (pd.DataFrame) – Input DataFrame containing a datetime column ‘MONTH_AND_DATE’.

Returns:

DataFrame with: - ‘day_of_week’: name of the weekday - ‘count’: number of dishes for each day Sorted in descending order of volume.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

data_analysis_package.data_analyzer_second_half.dish_volume_by_day(data_frame1: DataFrame) → Series[source]

Compute the average number of orders per dish per weekday.

Parameters:

data_frame1 (pd.DataFrame) – Input DataFrame containing: - ‘NAME_x’ (dish name) - ‘MONTH_AND_DATE’ (date) - ‘ORDERID’ (order identifier)

Returns:

Multi-index Series indexed by (dish, weekday), containing average number of orders, sorted descending.

Return type:

pd.Series
